title: symplocos pulchra subsp. hispidula
intro: Symplocos pulchra subsp. hispidula, commonly known as the Philippine syzygium, is a plant species belonging to the Symplocaceae family and is native to the Philippines. It is valued for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids in its leaves and bark. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, and is believed to provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and supporting cardiovascular health. Symplocos pulchra subsp. hispidula can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, or used as a topical application in herbal preparations.
